insight: A clock can guard the soul
plainSummary: For some minds, reflection becomes care only when it ends on time.
atAGlance: Some questions do not feel finished, even after they have taught enough. For people who chase perfect clarity, a set time can be kinder than a perfect answer. The aim is not to think less, but to stop feeding the chase. Hard grief, repair, and illness still need more help than a timer can give.
keyPoints: Set the close before you begin, then keep it.
The hunger for a perfect answer can become hidden avoidance.
Compare timed reflection with thinking until relief arrives.
